name: GPD Physics Benchmark — Quantum Information
description: Benchmark tasks from quantum information: entanglement, quantum circuits, error correction, and information-theoretic quantities.

tasks.title: Bell state entanglement entropy
tasks.description: Compute the entanglement entropy of a Bell state by tracing over one qubit.
tasks.problem_statement: For the Bell state |Phi+> = (|00> + |11>)/sqrt(2), compute the reduced density matrix rho_A by tracing over qubit B, and calculate the von Neumann entanglement entropy S(rho_A) = -Tr(rho_A ln rho_A).
conventions: Natural logarithm for entropy (units of nats); for bits use log base 2
tasks.expected_answer: rho_A = I/2 (maximally mixed state). S(rho_A) = ln 2 (in nats) = 1 bit.
verification_hints: Maximum entanglement for a two-qubit system is exactly ln 2 nats = 1 ebit
All four Bell states have the same entanglement entropy

tasks.title: No-cloning theorem
tasks.description: Prove that an unknown quantum state cannot be perfectly copied by a unitary operation.
tasks.problem_statement: Prove the no-cloning theorem: there is no unitary operator U acting on a system-ancilla Hilbert space H_A tensor H_B such that U|psi>|0> = |psi>|psi> for all |psi> in H_A.
given: Suppose U exists such that U|psi>|0> = |psi>|psi> for all |psi>
In particular: U|phi>|0> = |phi>|phi> for any other state |phi>
U is unitary: U^dagger U = I
assumptions: U is a valid unitary operation
conventions: |0> is the initial (blank) state of the ancilla
tasks.expected_answer: Proof by contradiction: Taking the inner product of U|psi>|0> = |psi>|psi> and U|phi>|0> = |phi>|phi>, unitarity gives <psi|phi> = (<psi|phi>)^2. This implies <psi|phi> = 0 or 1, so U can only clone states from an orthogonal set, not arbitrary states.
verification_hints: The key step: <psi|<0|U^dagger U|phi>|0> = <psi|phi> (unitarity) but also = (<psi|phi>)^2 (from the cloning hypothesis)
The equation x = x^2 has solutions x = 0 and x = 1 only
This means cloning works only for orthogonal states or identical states
Linearity of quantum mechanics is the fundamental obstacle to cloning

reference.title: A single quantum cannot be cloned

tasks.title: Quantum teleportation circuit verification
tasks.description: Verify that the standard quantum teleportation protocol correctly transfers an arbitrary qubit state.
tasks.problem_statement: Alice wants to teleport the state |psi> = alpha|0> + beta|1> to Bob using a shared Bell pair. Walk through the teleportation protocol: (1) Alice performs a CNOT then Hadamard on her qubits, (2) Alice measures both her qubits in the computational basis, (3) Bob applies corrections based on Alice's measurement outcome. Show that Bob's final state is |psi> for all four measurement outcomes.
given: Alice holds qubits 1 and 2; Bob holds qubit 3
assumptions: Classical communication channel from Alice to Bob
conventions: Pauli corrections: I, X, Z, XZ based on measurement outcomes 00, 01, 10, 11
tasks.expected_answer: After Alice's measurement of (m1, m2), Bob's state is: (00) -> alpha|0>+beta|1>, (01) -> alpha|1>+beta|0>, (10) -> alpha|0>-beta|1>, (11) -> alpha|1>-beta|0>. Applying X^{m2} Z^{m1} recovers |psi> in all cases.
verification_hints: Each measurement outcome occurs with probability 1/4
Without the classical communication (corrections), Bob's state is maximally mixed
The protocol uses 1 ebit + 2 classical bits to teleport 1 qubit
No information travels faster than light: Bob cannot determine his state without Alice's message

reference.title: Teleporting an unknown quantum state via dual classical and Einstein-Podolsky-Rosen channels
